flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, itemized units and members, disclosed effective date and service terms.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles together many named units (four infantry divisions plus numerous coast-artillery and observation-squadron units) into one order, but this is a single coherent mobilization action, not a bundling of unrelated policy grants.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Explicit twelve-consecutive-month term ('unless sooner relieved') — a genuine, disclosed bounding provision, distinguishing this from open-ended national-defense mechanisms.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; implements Congress's own contemporaneous authorization.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A major, historically resonant mobilization event — the first large-scale federalization of National Guard units in the pre-Pearl-Harbor buildup, relieving affected personnel from state control and placing them under Army governance for up to a year, part of the same 1940 national-defense-preparedness arc as the August cluster of personnel-flexibility orders (EO 8458, 8514, 8516).

title: Executive Order 8531—Amending Executive Order No. 7908 of June 9, 1938, Transferring Certain Lands to the Secretary of Agriculture for Use, Administration, and Disposition Under Title III of the Bankhead-Jones Farm Tenant Act

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Amendment that ADDS a check rather than removing one: newly requires the Secretary of the Interior's approval and a General Land Office patent before any Title III land may be disposed of, and carves out national-forest, wildlife-refuge, and mineral-resource interests from the order's reach — a protective, oversight-strengthening amendment.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed statutory basis, diff-aware full-text restatement of the amended provisos.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Strengthens rather than preempts oversight by adding an Interior Department approval and patent-issuance requirement.

title: Executive Order 8532—Amending Paragraph 1 of Executive Order No. 8458 of June 27, 1940, Directing the Civil Service Commission To Establish a Replacement List of Non-Civil Service Employees for Use for Temporary Appointments to National-Defense Positions

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Diff-aware narrowing amendment to the national-defense replacement-list mechanism: adds specific, bounded eligibility criteria (involuntary separation with good records on or after June 30, 1939, plus a minimum six-month prior service requirement) where the original order specified none — reduces rather than expands discretion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed, self-executing criteria.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Replaces open-ended eligibility with specific, checkable criteria (dates, service duration, record quality) — a vagueness-reducing amendment.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Remains tied to the bounded 'duration of the national-defense program' term already established in EO 8458, not independently expanded here.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Does not remove any pre-existing oversight; CSC continues to administer the list under tightened criteria.

title: Executive Order 8533—Designating the Secretary of the Treasury to Act in Respect of Any Bonds, Notes, or Other Securities Acquired on Behalf of the United States Under the Provisions of the Transportation Act, 1920, as Amended

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Designates the Secretary of the Treasury to exercise a specific securities-disposition authority newly created by a named August 1940 statute (Public No. 766) — implements rather than exceeds the statute's own grant, though the statutory discretion itself ('as the Secretary...may deem advisable') is broad.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed statutory basis; the discretionary language originates in the statute itself, not an independent expansion by this order.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; simply designates the officer to exercise a pre-existing statutory grant.

title: Executive Order 8534—Amending Schedules A and B of the Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Comprehensively restates and updates the government-wide civil-service-exemption schedules, but delegates the resulting discretion outward to the Civil Service Commission (which retains final decision authority and revocability) rather than concentrating power in the President himself.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Numerous categories throughout both schedules turn on undefined, self-certifying standards ('when in the opinion of the Commission it is not expedient,' 'not practicable,' 'impracticable') with no external check beyond the Commission's own judgment.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles together well over one hundred distinct, unrelated exemption categories spanning every executive department and dozens of independent agencies into a single consolidated order.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Pervasive vague, undefined qualifying standards ('not expedient,' 'not practicable,' 'best interests of the service so require') recur throughout both schedules with no operational criteria supplied.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Establishes a standing, indefinite-duration master exemption architecture with no sunset (one narrow FBI-related sub-provision aside), intended as permanent civil-service infrastructure.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The order itself preserves an institutional check: 'Final decision as to whether the duties of any position...are such that appointments thereto are authorized under any paragraph of Schedule A or B shall rest with the Civil Service Commission,' and revocation requires 'the concurrence of the department or agency concerned' — a real, if self-referential, retained gatekeeping structure.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: This is a comprehensive, foundational restatement of the master civil-service exemption schedule that governs hiring practices government-wide for years to come, not an isolated single-position grant.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: Definitionally the core exemptions-architecture instrument of this era: Schedules A and B ARE the master framework of civil-service exemptions, here comprehensively amended and consolidated across the entire executive branch, rather than a single bounded exemption grant — treated as PRESENT rather than CRITICAL because it updates a pre-existing, periodically-revised standing schedule (retaining CSC concurrence and revocability) rather than creating novel, unreviewable, permanent infrastructure of the kind that earned CRITICAL in the freezing-order family.

title: Executive Order 8535—Amending the Foreign Service Regulations of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Trivial technical text amendment to Foreign Service Regulations (substituting 'Attorney General' for 'Secretary of Labor' and adding a consular-agents carve-out).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, minor, self-executing.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8536—Establishing San Clemente Island Naval Defensive Sea Area

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Diff-aware expansion of a standing defensive-sea-area exclusion zone (California) from 300 yards to a full nautical mile, with the Secretary of the Navy retaining sole, unilateral authority to permit vessel entry — matching this stream's established defensive-sea-area family (cf. EO 8143, EO 8403).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named authority (Secretary of the Navy) is clearly disclosed on the order's face.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Enforcement is left to generic 'penalties provided by law' with no specification of the applicable statute or penalty scope.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No expiration or review mechanism — a standing, indefinite-duration exclusion zone, matching the established defensive-sea-area family.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sole gatekeeping authority rests with the Secretary of the Navy's own discretion, with no other described check on entry authorization.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically resonant national-defense maritime exclusion zone in the immediate pre-Pearl-Harbor buildup, matching the established defensive-sea-area family (EO 8143 Pearl Harbor, EO 8403 Los Angeles-Long Beach Harbor).

title: Executive Order 8537—Authorizing Appointment of Certain Experts in the Antitrust Division, Department of Justice, Without Regard to the Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Individually-named appointment bypass covering sixteen disclosed experts to a disclosed division.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Sixteen named individuals, disclosed division; but no recommending official or other accountability anchor is disclosed anywhere in the order.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Bypasses competitive examination for sixteen appointments with no disclosed justification or accountability anchor.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No stated end date or bounding condition on the appointments.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No recommending official or other accountability anchor is cited anywhere in the order, matching this stream's unmitigated bypass template; the order does include a genuine downstream limitation — appointees do not thereby acquire competitive classified status — which caps but does not eliminate the structural gap.

title: Executive Order 8538—Exemption of Z. Lewis Dalby From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Individually-named, one-year retirement-age exemption, matching this stream's established template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed one-year end date (September 30, 1941) — bounded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8539—Exemption of Herbert A. Howell From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Individually-named, one-year retirement-age exemption, matching this stream's established template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed one-year end date (September 30, 1941) — bounded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8540—Revoking in Part Executive Order No. 8344 of February 10, 1940, and Reserving Public Land for Use as an Air Navigation Site

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, single-16.98-acre partial revocation and air-navigation-site reservation (Alaska), subject to valid existing rights.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, precise metes-and-bounds description, self-executing, diff-aware.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8541—Designation of the Assistant Secretary of Commerce and the Solicitor of Commerce To Act as Secretary of Commerce

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Routine statutory succession designation implementing 5 U.S.C. sec. 6, superseding one named 1938 predecessor order.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed order of succession, self-executing.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8542—Authorizing the Appointment of Harold Ambrose to a Classified Position in the Post Office Department Without Regard to the Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, single individually-named status-conversion bypass to a disclosed classified position.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed department, and a disclosed, verifiable seven-year service history ('since June 12, 1933, has been serving in a position...excepted from civil service requirements') functioning as a substitute accountability anchor documenting an established track record.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Bypasses the competitive examination normally required for classified status, though converting an already long-serving excepted employee rather than a fresh outside appointment — a distinct 'long-tenure status-conversion' bypass sub-variant.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Once granted, classified civil-service status is permanent with no stated bounding condition.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The disclosed seven-year prior service history in the same department functions as a substitute accountability anchor, distinguishing this from the unmitigated bypass template that lacks any disclosed track record.

title: Executive Order 8543—Exemption of Samuel J. Gompers From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Individually-named, third consecutive retirement-age exemption cycle (cumulative two years across EO 7958 and EO 8252), matching this stream's established template; diff-aware citation to both predecessor orders.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term, diff-aware.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed one-year end date (September 30, 1941) — bounded, each grant remains fixed-term regardless of cumulative renewal.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8544—Transfer of Lands From the Lolo National Forest to the Helena National Forest

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, boundary-adjustment land transfer between two national forests (Montana), upon the Secretary of Agriculture's recommendation, with an explicit protective disclaimer on its face ('It is not intended by this order to give a national-forest status to any publicly-owned lands which do not now have such status, or to remove any publicly-owned lands from a national-forest status').

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, disclosed recommender, precise boundary description, disclosed protective disclaimer.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; the disclosed disclaimer is itself a status-quo-preserving feature.

title: Executive Order 8545—Selective Service Regulations (Volumes One and Two: Organization and Administration; Registration)

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Vests the Director of Selective Service, 'responsible directly to the President,' with broad self-executing authority to 'prescribe such amendments to these regulations as he shall deem necessary,' issue binding public notices/orders/instructions, appoint officers and employees, and delegate his functions — substantial rule-making and personnel discretion concentrated in a single executive-branch official implementing the newly-enacted Selective Training and Service Act of 1940.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extensive, disclosed accountability architecture built into the machinery itself: mandatory chain-of-command reporting (registrar to county clerk to Governor to Director), readiness telegrams, itemized registration counts, mandatory record-keeping and receipting for every custody transfer of registration cards, sworn oaths for every official (Form 21), and a detailed confidential-records regime specifying exactly who may access sensitive files and under what conditions.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single, coherent implementation of one statute (the Selective Training and Service Act of 1940) — organization, definitions, and registration procedures for one integrated system, not a bundling of unrelated policy grants.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Criminal penalties for evasion, false statements, or obstruction are specifically defined by direct citation to Section 11 of the Act ('imprisonment for not more than five years or a fine of not more than $10,000'), not left to undefined administrative discretion.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No termination date or review trigger is stated for the administrative machinery established by this order itself; the resulting Selective Service System became durable, decades-long federal infrastructure.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Board of Appeal classification decisions are made 'final, unless modified or reversed by the President' — this order centralizes ultimate appellate authority in the President alone, with no other described check (judicial or congressional) on its face.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Establishes, for the first time, the complete administrative machinery of America's first peacetime military draft — local boards, boards of appeal, and the registration/records apparatus that would determine the military status of millions of American men — foundational and enduring institutional infrastructure rather than merely a historically resonant single action, matching the CRITICAL standard set by the freezing-order family (EO 8389 et seq.) for foundational, permanent-infrastructure-creating orders.

title: Executive Order 8546—Creating the Defense Communications Board and Defining Its Functions and Duties

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Creates an interagency contingency-planning board (FCC Chairman, Army Signal Officer, Director of Naval Communications, State and Treasury representatives) under the Communications Act of 1934 to develop plans for wartime communications coordination; the Board's function is expressly limited to planning and recommendation, with 'reports containing the findings and recommendations of the Board' submitted 'to the President for final action' — deliberative rather than an operational grant of unilateral control.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Membership, chairmanship succession order, and reporting channel to the President are all disclosed on the order's face.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single-subject creation of one coordinating board, not a bundling of unrelated grants.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing board with no stated expiration or review trigger.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The order includes a genuine, disclosed self-limiting proviso on its face: 'The Board shall take no cognizance of matters pertaining to censorship' — explicitly excluding the most oversight-sensitive communications-control function from the Board's scope rather than removing any existing check.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Direct institutional precursor to the wartime U.S. communications-control apparatus that would follow within roughly a year (the Office of Censorship and expanded FCC wartime authority) — historically resonant early-warning infrastructure in the pre-Pearl-Harbor defense-preparedness buildup.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Coordinates rather than pits the represented agencies (FCC, Army, Navy, State, Treasury/Coast Guard) against one another over communications resources — cooperative planning, not cannibalization.

title: Executive Order 8547—Amending the Foreign Service Regulations of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Technical amendment adding a new Foreign Service Regulations chapter (catastrophe reporting, rescue reporting, distressed-citizen assistance) that principally RESTRICTS officer conduct — explicitly prohibiting personal financial aid, personal credit obligations, and (outside public emergencies) acceptance of private property for safekeeping — an oversight-strengthening, not oversight-weakening, amendment.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed, diff-aware (renumbers sections, cancels named superseded provisions, revokes one named 1926 order).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Adds restrictions on officer conduct rather than removing any pre-existing check.

title: Executive Order 8548—Establishing the North Carolina Wildlife Management Area

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Large-acreage (58,900 acres) but standard wildlife-refuge template, North Carolina, upon the Secretary of Agriculture's recommendation, with a disclosed cooperative-use proviso preserving the State's continued management under an existing license agreement and preserving Agriculture's jurisdiction over lands still being acquired.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, disclosed recommender, precise acreage and county boundaries, subject to valid existing rights.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8549—Designating the Secretary of the Treasury To Accept on Behalf of the United States a Certain Tract of Land Authorized To Be Conveyed to the United States by the Legislature of Puerto Rico

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Routine designation of the Secretary of the Treasury to accept a specific land conveyance (Puerto Rico, 4.48 acres) implementing a named 1917 statute and a named 1940 Puerto Rico legislative act.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, precise metes-and-bounds description, disclosed purpose (Coast Guard use).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8550—Modification of Executive Order No. 3271 of May 11, 1920, Withdrawing Public Lands

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow modification (Utah) permitting the Secretary of the Interior to consummate three named land-exchange applications under a 1929 Public Resolution, to effectuate an established 1928 Bear River Migratory Bird Refuge statute.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, disclosed application numbers, diff-aware.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8551—Ordering Certain Units and Members of the National Guard of the United States Into the Active Military Service of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Second National Guard federalization order in this stream (matching EO 8530), federalizing three additional infantry divisions plus supporting units, pursuant to the same explicit congressional authorization (Public Resolution No. 96) and the 1916 National Defense Act.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, itemized units and members, disclosed effective date and service terms.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles many named units into one order, but this is a single coherent mobilization action, not a bundling of unrelated policy grants, matching the treatment of EO 8530.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Explicit twelve-consecutive-month term ('unless sooner relieved') — a genuine, disclosed bounding provision, matching EO 8530.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; implements Congress's own contemporaneous authorization.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Second instance in a rapidly-forming pattern of mass National Guard federalization (EO 8530, and now this order, five weeks later) — part of the same historically resonant pre-Pearl-Harbor mobilization arc.

title: Executive Order 8552—Exemption of George W. Stose From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Individually-named, second consecutive one-year retirement-age exemption, matching this stream's established template; diff-aware citation to EO 8268.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term, diff-aware.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed one-year end date (October 31, 1941) — bounded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8553—Designating Lieutenant Colonel Lewis B. Hershey To Perform Certain Duties Under the Selective Training and Service Act of 1940

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, explicitly bounded interim designation ('pending the appointment of a Director of Selective Service') of a single named Army officer to perform the Director's administrative duties already defined in EO 8545 — a bridging, transitional designation rather than an independent grant of new power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, named officer, disclosed transitional scope tied to a specific, already-defined duty set (paragraph 119, Section III, Volume One).

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Expressly self-terminating upon the appointment of a permanent Director — a genuine, disclosed bounding condition.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; operates entirely within the already-disclosed Selective Service Regulations framework.

title: Executive Order 8554—Exemption of William T. Andrews From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Individually-named, second consecutive one-year retirement-age exemption, matching this stream's established template; diff-aware citation to EO 8228.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term, diff-aware.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed one-year end date (September 30, 1941) — bounded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8555—Exemption of Samuel A. Cottrell From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Individually-named, second consecutive retirement-age exemption for a partial nine-month period, matching this stream's established template; diff-aware citation to EO 8262.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed term, diff-aware.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed nine-month end date (June 30, 1941) — bounded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8556—Transferring the Use, Possession, and Control of Certain Lands to the Tennessee Valley Authority

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, tract-specific interagency land transfer (Alabama, ~49 acres, Elk River Shoals Canal) from the War Department to TVA, implementing a specific 1933 statutory provision, disclosed as no longer needed following completion of the Wheeler Dam.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, precise metes-and-bounds description, disclosed rationale.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8557—Prescribing Regulations Governing the Payment of Expenses Incurred in Connection With the Death of Certain Civilian Officers and Employees of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Comprehensive but single-subject implementation of a specific July 1940 death-benefits statute (Public No. 729), detailing remains preparation/transportation, dependent and household-effects transportation, and payment procedures — administers rather than exceeds the statute's own grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Highly detailed, itemized, transparent criteria throughout (dollar caps, weight/measurement tables by grade, one-year shipment time limits), including an explicit anti-double-payment safeguard ('No payment shall be made...in any case where payment therefor is authorized by any other law').

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single coherent regulatory scheme implementing one statute, not a bundling of unrelated grants.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Specific, itemized numeric and procedural standards throughout, not vague catch-all criteria.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing peacetime benefits-administration regulation, no crisis tie, matching this stream's convention for ordinary administrative frameworks (cf. EO 8512).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Does not remove a pre-existing check; implements the statute's own benefit scheme with a built-in anti-double-payment safeguard.

title: Executive Order 8558—Transferring Certain Lands to the Secretary of Agriculture for Use, Administration, and Disposition Under Title III of the Bankhead-Jones Farm Tenant Act

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, tract-specific land transfer (South Carolina, ~167 acres, Saluda Garden Homes Project) implementing a specific 1937 statute, matching this stream's established Bankhead-Jones land-transfer template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, precise deed citations and acreage.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8559—Designating Colonel Lewis B. Hershey To Perform Certain Duties Under the Selective Training and Service Act of 1940

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Broadens Hershey's interim authority beyond EO 8553 to include appointing 'necessary members of local boards, local board physicians, government appeal agents, and members of appeal boards' — delegating the President's own statutory appointment power over the entire initial nationwide network of draft-administration personnel to a single named officer, though still explicitly framed as transitional ('pending the appointment of a Director of Selective Service').

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named officer, disclosed scope tied to specific already-published regulation sections (Sections V and VI, Volume One), and the underlying regulations themselves continue to require gubernatorial recommendation for each appointment.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Expressly self-terminating upon the appointment of a permanent Director — a genuine, disclosed bounding condition, matching EO 8553.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The delegated appointment authority continues to operate within the Selective Service Regulations' existing gubernatorial-recommendation constraint; no check is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: One-time, bounded institutional bootstrapping of the entire initial nationwide Selective Service local-board and appeal-board personnel network — a significant staffing action for a newly-created institution, matching this stream's 'new-institution initial-staffing bypass' family (cf. EO 8334).

title: Executive Order 8560—Selective Service Regulations (Volume Three: Classification and Selection)

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes the actual draft classification/deferment machinery, but decision-making is genuinely distributed: initial classification rests with civilian-volunteer local boards, review with boards of appeal, conscientious-objector determinations are referred to the Department of Justice for independent inquiry and hearing, and presidential appeal is narrowly confined to dependency-hardship cases meeting five explicit disclosed conditions ('A board of appeal must have...at least one or more members...dissented...on grounds of dependency only...') — a checked, dispersed structure rather than concentration in the President or Director.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Remarkably extensive, disclosed procedural safeguards for a conscription-classification regime: mandatory written notice at every classification step, subpoena power exercised through the courts, a registrant's guaranteed right to examine his own file, an explicit non-discrimination mandate on its face ('There shall be no discrimination for or against any person because of his race, creed, or color, or because of his membership or activity in any labor, political, religious, or other organization'), and independent Department of Justice hearings for conscientious-objector claims.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single, coherent implementation of one statute's classification scheme (Classes I through IV and their subclasses), not a bundling of unrelated policy grants.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: The pivotal occupational-deferment standard turns on open-ended, subjective criteria — a registrant is a 'necessary man' only if 'his removal would cause a material loss of effectiveness' in an activity 'useful or productive' to 'the national health, safety, or interest' — and the Class IV-F 'morally unfit' determination is left to whatever felony conviction 'the local board determines renders the registrant morally unfit,' both undefined operative standards controlling whether a man is drafted.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No termination date is stated for the classification machinery established by this order; ties into the same durable, standing Selective Service System infrastructure as EO 8545.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The order explicitly eliminates a normally-expected due-process safeguard at the pivotal initial classification stage: 'No registrant may be represented before the local board by an attorney' — a disclosed, concrete removal of legal representation during the proceeding that determines a man's liability for military service, only partially offset by the appeal, DOJ-referral, and narrow presidential-appeal channels that do exist.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Directly determines, for millions of registrants, who serves, who is deferred, and who is exempted in the largest mobilization in American history to that point — foundational and enduring in the same category as the freezing-order family and EO 8545's organizational machinery, not merely a historically resonant single action.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: Establishes the master exemption/deferment classification architecture for the WWII draft — four classes and eleven subclasses (occupational necessity, dependency, ministerial status, alien status, physical/mental/moral fitness, conscientious objection) — a novel, foundational, life-and-death exemptions schedule with no comparable predecessor, distinguished from EO 8534's Schedule A/B (PRESENT) by the absence of any pre-existing periodically-revised framework or retained-concurrence/revocability language; this is treated as CRITICAL independently of Flag 9 because it satisfies its own operational test — the existence of a comprehensive, novel exemption architecture — rather than merely restating the same downstream-effects basis.

title: Executive Order 8561—Selective Service Regulations (Volume Five: Finance)

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Comprehensive but purely fiscal/procurement administration for the Selective Service System, implementing the same 1940 Act; the Director's spending authority is expressly 'lawful expenditures as he deems necessary' within congressionally-appropriated limits, not an independent grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extraordinarily itemized, transparent accountability structure throughout: mandatory monthly expense estimates and obligation reports, dollar-capped office-assistant pay schedules keyed to registrant counts, mandatory certification language on every bill/invoice/voucher ('I certify that the above bill is correct and just...'), quarterly property accountability reports, and dual civilian/military cross-checks (State procurement officer plus Chief of Finance, U.S. Army, as fiscal agent).

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single-subject financial/procurement administration for one system, not a bundling of unrelated grants.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Specific, itemized dollar caps, time limits, and procedures throughout (e.g., temporary-employee pay capped at '$1 per hour or $5 in any one day'), not vague catch-all criteria.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing peacetime financial-administration regulation, no independent crisis discretion, matching this stream's convention for ordinary administrative frameworks (cf. EO 8512, EO 8557).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing check is removed; layered certification and dual-officer accountability are built into the machinery itself.

title: Executive Order 8562—Exemption of James L. Hughes From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Individually-named, second consecutive one-year retirement-age exemption, matching this stream's established template; diff-aware citation to EO 8260.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term, diff-aware.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed one-year end date (November 30, 1941) — bounded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8563—Amendment of Subdivision VII, Schedule A, Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, single-category civil-service exemption addition for temporary holiday-season Postal Service workers, expressly bounded to a specific two-month window ('from November 15, 1940, to January 15, 1941').

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed, self-executing, narrow scope.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Explicit, disclosed calendar bounding dates — a genuine sunset, distinguishing this from the standing exemption mechanisms elsewhere in this stream (EO 8534, EO 8576).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: Too narrow and explicitly time-bound to constitute standing exemptions architecture in the sense that fires this flag elsewhere in this stream (cf. EO 8534, EO 8560, EO 8576); a single seasonal carve-out, not a comprehensive framework.

title: Executive Order 8564—Amendment of Executive Order No. 8257 of September 21, 1939, Authorizing Excepted Appointments To Meet Public Exigency

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Diff-aware restatement of the standing 'general prospective exigency-bypass authorization' first established in EO 8257: delegates to the Civil Service Commission alone the power to authorize bypassing competitive examination requirements government-wide for any position 'connected with the national-defense program,' re-scoping (from 'neutrality...or preparedness program' to specifically 'national-defense program') but not narrowing the underlying standing bypass mechanism.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The triggering determination ('the Civil Service Commission determines that it is in the public interest to do so') is entirely self-certifying, with no external check disclosed on the Commission's own judgment of what constitutes a qualifying 'public exigency.'

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Public exigency directly connected with the national-defense program' and 'urgency for the immediate filling of a position' are undefined, broad, subjective triggering standards controlling access to a government-wide bypass mechanism.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Tied to the open-ended 'duration of the national-defense program,' with no fixed end date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Retains two genuine, disclosed limiting provisos carried over from the original EO 8257 text: appointees remain subject to 'noncompetitive tests of fitness as the Commission may prescribe,' and 'persons so appointed will not thereby acquire a classified (competitive) civil service status' — real, disclosed safeguards distinguishing this from an unmitigated bypass.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: This is the umbrella standing mechanism directly cited by name and relied upon elsewhere in this stream's corpus (e.g., EO 8561, paragraph 553, invokes it directly for Selective Service inspector/coordinator hiring) — real, ongoing structural reliance across the wartime-preparedness hiring infrastructure, not an isolated provision.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: A standing, government-wide bypass mechanism rather than a single bounded grant, matching this stream's exemptions-architecture treatment for EO 8534.

title: Executive Order 8566—Amending the Foreign Service Regulations of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow technical amendment directing Foreign Service officers to perform specific alien-admission duties for the Philippine Islands under a named local statute, revoking one named 1935 predecessor order.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed, diff-aware.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8567—Providing for the Administration of the Act Entitled "An Act To Authorize the President To Requisition Certain Articles and Materials for the Use of the United States, and for Other Purposes"

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements a brand-new congressional grant of requisitioning authority (approved just five days earlier) by distributing administration between the War and Navy Secretaries acting JOINTLY through the Army and Navy Munitions Board, and the Administrator of Export Control for remaining provisions — a distributive, dual-officer implementation rather than a unilateral or independently expanded grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The joint-determination requirement between two Cabinet officers, exercised through a named institutional board, is itself a disclosed, mutual accountability structure.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Necessity of requisitioning...in the interest of national defense' supplies no defined criteria for what materials or quantities qualify.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No termination date is stated; tied to the open-ended national-defense need.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The joint War-Navy determination requirement, exercised through the Army and Navy Munitions Board, functions as a genuine, disclosed cross-check on any individual requisitioning decision.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Direct institutional precursor to the WWII industrial-mobilization requisitioning apparatus, implementing brand-new emergency legislation only five days after its enactment — historically resonant early defense-preparedness infrastructure.

title: Executive Order 8568—Establishing the St. Lawrence Advisory Committee and Providing for a Preliminary Investigation of International Rapids Section, St. Lawrence River

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Creates an advisory/investigatory committee (FPC Chairman, Assistant Secretary of State, an Army Corps of Engineers general, and a New York Power Authority representative) whose function is expressly confined to preliminary investigation and recommendation, with 'periodic reports, with recommendations to the President' — deliberative, not an operational grant of construction or control authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Membership and reporting channel to the President are disclosed on the order's face.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Grants the Federal Power Commission and Army Corps of Engineers blanket authority to 'appoint, without regard to the civil service laws, such officers, experts, and employees as they may deem necessary,' and to 'prescribe their functions, duties, responsibilities and tenure' — an open-ended staffing bypass for an entire new investigatory effort, with no recommender or anchor disclosed.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No stated end date for the bypass hiring authority or the Committee itself.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No described check (CSC approval, recommender, or otherwise) on the exercise of the civil-service-bypass hiring authority — matching this stream's 'new-institution initial-staffing bypass' family (cf. EO 8334).

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Direct institutional seed of the eventual St. Lawrence Seaway project — a decades-spanning infrastructure initiative — begun here as a modest preliminary-investigation committee justified by national-security/defense framing.

title: Executive Order 8569—Designating the Director of Selective Service to Perform Certain Duties and Functions Under the Selective Training and Service Act of 1940

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Delegates the President's own statutory appointment power over the entire nationwide network of local-board members, physicians, government appeal agents, and appeal-board members to the (now presumably permanently appointed) Director of Selective Service on a STANDING basis — unlike the explicitly transitional EO 8553/EO 8559 predecessors ('pending the appointment of a Director'), this order carries no such bounding language.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The delegated appointment authority continues to operate within the Selective Service Regulations' existing gubernatorial-recommendation constraint (Sections V and VI, Volume One).

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Unlike EO 8553 and EO 8559, this order contains no 'pending the appointment of a Director' or other bounding/self-terminating language — a standing delegation with no stated review trigger.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Continues to operate within the underlying Regulations' gubernatorial-recommendation requirement; no check is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Completes the institutionalization of the permanent nationwide Selective Service local-board appointment structure, following the bounded interim delegations of EO 8553/EO 8559.

title: Executive Order 8570—Selective Service Regulations (Volume Six: Physical Standards)

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Adopts, by direct incorporation, the existing published physical-examination standards of the War and Navy Departments ('Standards of Physical Examinations during Mobilization, MR 1-9, War Dept. Aug. 31, 1940') rather than creating independent new standards; confirms and cross-references the already-distributed local-board/medical-advisory-board classification structure established in EO 8560.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Detailed confidentiality and record-handling rules carried forward from Volume One (confidential-records access list, registrant's guaranteed right to know his own record).

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single-subject medical-protocol volume implementing one statute, cross-referencing rather than duplicating or expanding the classification architecture already scored in EO 8560.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Physical fitness classification is anchored to established, externally-published military medical standards, not undefined administrative discretion.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Procedural/medical-protocol volume with no independent discretionary grant; tied to the same underlying Act as the already-scored EO 8545/EO 8560.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; 'the final decision as to the acceptance or rejection of men...rests with the examining physicians at induction stations,' a disclosed final-check structure.

title: Executive Order 8571—Transfer of Control and Jurisdiction Over Certain Lands From the Secretary of Agriculture to the Secretary of the Interior

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, tract-specific interagency land transfer (Oklahoma, 4.43 acres) implementing a specific 1937 statute, upon the Secretary of Agriculture's recommendation, for the benefit of designated Indians via the Commissioner of Indian Affairs.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, disclosed recommender, precise metes-and-bounds description.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8572—Authorizing the Priorities Board and the Administrator of Priorities To Perform Certain Functions Under Section 2 (a) of the Act of June 28, 1940

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Approves a Board (already convened by the Council of National Defense) and a named Administrator (Donald M. Nelson) with authority to REQUIRE private companies holding Army/Navy contracts to grant delivery priority over private-account or export deliveries — a significant new command-economy-style mechanism over private industry, directly implementing a specific June 1940 statutory grant of Presidential discretion rather than exceeding it.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The Board's operative 'rules and regulations' require disclosed Presidential approval before taking effect — a genuine, built-in check on the scope of the Board's exercised authority.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Acting in the public interest and in the interest of the national defense' supplies no defined criteria for which contracts or deliveries qualify for priority treatment.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No termination date stated; tied to the open-ended national-defense need.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The requirement that governing rules and regulations be approved by the President is a genuine, disclosed check retained on the Board's exercise of this authority.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Direct institutional precursor to the WWII War Production Board / wartime priorities-and-allocations system — the named Administrator, Donald Nelson, would go on to chair the WPB in 1942 — historically resonant early industrial-mobilization infrastructure.

title: Executive Order 8573—Reserving Public Land for Town-Site Purposes

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, single-tract public-land reservation (Alaska, Unalaska, 100 acres) for future disposal under existing town-site laws.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, precise metes-and-bounds description, self-executing.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8574—Selective Service Regulations (Volume Four: Delivery and Induction)

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Purely mechanical quota, call, and delivery procedures for the already-established classification system (EO 8560); the Governor's 'call' authority is itself bounded ('No call made on a local board shall be for more than the board's current net quota').

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Extraordinarily itemized, transparent procedures: numbered calls, telegraphic reporting requirements, mandatory record transfers at every step (Forms 149-167), and an explicit safeguard that 'no registrant shall be ordered to report for induction while there is pending any appeal.'

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single-subject procedural implementation of one statute's induction machinery, not a bundling of unrelated grants.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Highly specific, itemized numeric and procedural quota-computation rules, not vague catch-all criteria.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Procedural volume with no independent discretionary grant beyond what is already scored in EO 8545/EO 8560.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; explicitly preserves the pending-appeal induction stay already established in EO 8560.

title: Executive Order 8575—Establishing the Thief Valley National Wildlife Refuge

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard wildlife-refuge template (Oregon, ~1,495 acres), with a disclosed compatibility proviso preserving the land's continued use for the Baker Reclamation Project ('without interference with their use for such reclamation purposes').

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, precise metes-and-bounds description, disclosed compatibility proviso.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8576—Labor Regulations

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Comprehensive standing personnel-architecture for the 'unclassified laborer' category of federal employment, but authority is delegated outward to and administered by the Civil Service Commission throughout (ratings, certification, reinstatement limits, transfers), not concentrated in the President.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Regulation XI's removal standard is explicitly discretionary and due-process-light on its face: 'no trial or hearing shall be required except at the discretion of such [employing] officer' — though tempered by a disclosed record-keeping and CSC-inspection requirement ('reasons for each removal shall be made of record...and shall be open to the inspection of the Commission').

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single-subject comprehensive regulation of one employment category (unclassified laborers), matching this stream's treatment of EO 8534 and EO 8560 as non-bundling.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: The removal standard — 'such cause as in the judgment of the employing officer will promote the efficiency of the service' — is a broad, subjective criterion controlling termination for an entire category of federal workers.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, permanent regulatory architecture (expressly supersedes a 1922 predecessor order), no expiration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing check is removed; CSC retains administration, inspection rights over removal records, and certification authority throughout.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Comprehensive, government-wide standing personnel architecture for the entire federal manual-labor workforce, with real and lasting institutional reach, though narrower in stakes than EO 8560's draft-classification architecture.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: Comprehensively defines and consolidates an entire 'unclassified' personnel category exempted from ordinary classified/competitive status rules — matching this stream's treatment of EO 8534 (PRESENT, not CRITICAL, given it restates and supersedes a pre-existing 1922 framework with retained CSC administration rather than creating novel, unreviewable architecture).

title: Executive Order 8577—Withdrawing Public Land For Use of the War Department

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, single-1.2-acre public-land withdrawal (Alaska, Kotzebue) for a War Department radio-station site, subject to valid existing rights.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, precise metes-and-bounds description, self-executing.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8578—Withdrawal of Public Land for Use of the War Department as an Aerial Bombing and Gunnery Range

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Very large-acreage (~3,560,000 acres) but standard aerial-bombing-range land withdrawal, Nevada, matching this stream's established large-acreage-withdrawal-scored-clean precedent (cf. EO 8507); scale alone not treated as indicating structural risk for a single consistent action.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, precise boundary description, disclosed subject-to-valid-existing-rights proviso.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; the order explicitly preserves rather than rescinds two named prior wildlife-protection withdrawal orders ('shall take precedence over, but shall not rescind or revoke...the withdrawals made by Executive Order No. 6910...and Executive Order No. 7373...for wildlife and other purposes') — a genuine, disclosed non-disruptive proviso.

title: Executive Order 8579—Withdrawal of Public Land for Use of the War Department as an Aerial Bombing and Gunnery Range

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Very large-acreage (~1,560,000 acres) but standard aerial-bombing-range land withdrawal, Utah, matching the same precedent applied to EO 8578.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, precise boundary description, disclosed subject-to-valid-existing-rights proviso.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; explicitly preserves rather than rescinds three named prior orders (water reserves and air-navigation facilities) — a genuine, disclosed non-disruptive proviso, matching EO 8578.

title: Executive Order 8581—Exemption of Andrew McClellan From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Individually-named, one-year retirement-age exemption, matching this stream's established template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed one-year end date (November 30, 1941) — bounded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8582—Exemption of Hervey G. Mooney From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Individually-named, one-year retirement-age exemption, matching this stream's established template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed one-year end date (October 31, 1941) — bounded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8583—Partial Revocation of Executive Order No. 5886 of July 12, 1932, Withdrawing Public Lands

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, single-township land-withdrawal revocation (Wyoming), effective only upon official filing of a resurvey plat — a self-limiting, conditional trigger.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, self-executing, disclosed conditional effective date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; restores land to ordinary status, a power-narrowing feature.

title: Executive Order 8584—Exemption of Frank Burke From Compulsory Retirement for Age

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Individually-named, second consecutive one-year retirement-age exemption, matching this stream's established template; diff-aware citation to EO 8302.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named individual, disclosed position, fixed one-year term, diff-aware.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Fixed one-year end date (December 31, 1941) — bounded.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8585—Amending Paragraph 7, Subdivision I, Schedule A of the Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow technical update to a single pre-existing Schedule A exemption paragraph, clarifying its application to the Philippine Islands — a textual clarification of an already-existing exemption category (part of the Schedule A architecture already scored at EO 8534), not an independent new grant of exemption authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed, self-executing.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: A narrow textual clarification of one paragraph within the already-scored Schedule A architecture (EO 8534), not an independent exemption grant warranting separate firing of this flag.

title: Executive Order 8586—Authorizing the Inspection by Receivers, and Trustees in Bankruptcy...of Certain Tax Returns

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends existing Treasury tax-return-inspection regulations (already approved by the President in 1938/1939/1940 Treasury decisions) to a new category of requesters (receivers and bankruptcy trustees), implementing over a dozen specifically-cited statutory provisions rather than creating independent new disclosure authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Compliance is expressly conditioned on 'rules and regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury' in named, dated Treasury decisions — a disclosed, itemized administrative framework.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; extends an existing regulated-access framework rather than creating unchecked disclosure.

title: Executive Order 8587—Amending Certain Provisions of the Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Comprehensively rewrites ten separate Civil Service Rules (I through X), but delegates the resulting administrative discretion outward to the Civil Service Commission throughout, rather than concentrating power in the President.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Contains unusually strong, disclosed transparency features: the 'rare cases' noncompetitive-appointment bypass (Rule II, sec. 8) requires a 'detailed statement of the reasons for its action...made in the records of the Commission and...published in its annual report' — genuine public accountability rarely seen elsewhere in this stream's bypass mechanisms — plus an explicit non-discrimination clause (political/religious opinion) and an anti-coercion provision protecting applicants from being pressured to withdraw.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles together substantive amendments to appointment, examination, transfer, promotion, probation, reinstatement, and merit-selection procedures across ten distinct Civil Service Rules into a single order — genuinely disparate procedural domains, not a single coherent subject.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Numerous provisions turn on undefined Commission discretion ('in its judgment,' 'in its discretion,' 'when in its opinion...not practicable'), including the core 'rare cases' bypass trigger itself.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing, permanent rules architecture with no expiration, governing ongoing civil-service administration indefinitely.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The 'rare cases' bypass mechanism (Rule II, sec. 8) includes a genuine, disclosed external-transparency safeguard — mandatory publication of reasons in the Commission's annual report, plus a requirement that any SUBSEQUENT vacancy in the same position return to competitive examination absent express Commission authority — materially distinguishing this from the unmitigated bypass template used elsewhere in this stream.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A comprehensive, foundational rewrite of the operative procedural rules governing the entire federal classified civil service — several provisions (the non-discrimination clause, the anti-coercion clause) are recognizable direct ancestors of modern merit-system-protection law.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: Formalizes and generalizes several standing exemption/bypass mechanisms government-wide (excepted-to-classified conversion, rare-cases noncompetitive appointment, service-beyond-seas classified status, a defense-program merit-selection carve-out in Rule VII), matching this stream's PRESENT (not CRITICAL) treatment of EO 8534, given the genuine disclosed safeguards (annual publication, bounded time limits, Commission review) distinguishing it from novel, unreviewable architecture.

title: Executive Order 8588—Prescribing Regulations Governing the Payment of Expenses of Transportation of Household Goods and Personal Effects of Certain Civilian Officers and Employees of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Comprehensive but single-subject implementation of a specific October 1940 statute (Public No. 839), detailing weight/volume allowances by dependency status, packing/drayage/shipment rules, and time limits — administers rather than exceeds the statute's own grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Highly detailed, itemized, transparent criteria throughout (specific weight tables, dollar-per-hundred-pound caps, six-month/two-year shipment time limits), with an explicit 'transfer for convenience of employee' disqualification safeguard preventing abuse.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single coherent regulatory scheme implementing one statute, not a bundling of unrelated grants.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Specific, itemized numeric and procedural standards throughout, not vague catch-all criteria.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing peacetime benefits-administration regulation, no crisis tie, matching this stream's convention (cf. EO 8512, EO 8557).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Does not remove a pre-existing check; implements the statute's own benefit scheme with a built-in anti-abuse safeguard.

title: Executive Order 8589—Modifying Executive Order of October 27, 1914, Creating Power Site Reserve No. 461

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, single-highway right-of-way modification (Idaho) to a standing power-site reserve, bounded by a discontinuance condition if found in conflict with authorized project works.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, tract-specific, self-executing, disclosed discontinuance condition.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; the discontinuance condition is itself a protective, power-narrowing feature.

title: Executive Order 8590—Fixing the Number of Men To Be Inducted Into the Land Forces of the United States Prior to July 1, 1941, and Directing Their Selection and Induction

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The first numeric exercise of the already-established Selective Service classification/induction machinery (EO 8545, 8560, 8574): sets a fixed cap (800,000), and directs the Secretary of War and Director of Selective Service to act 'in the manner provided in the Selective Training and Service Act...and the regulations issued thereunder' — fully bounded by, and does not modify, the already-scored procedural architecture.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed numeric cap and statutory basis; execution is delegated to already-disclosed, already-scored procedures.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Explicitly bounded to a fixed cap (800,000 men) and a fixed date (prior to July 1, 1941) — a genuine, disclosed limiting provision, notable given this is the historic first activation of the draft machinery at scale.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion or check-removal; execution remains fully governed by the already-scored EO 8545/8560/8574 procedures, including their disclosed appeal and due-process features.

title: Executive Order 8591—Withdrawal of Public Land in Aid of Flood Control

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Land withdrawal (Arkansas, ~4,260 acres) for the Norfork Dam and Reservoir project, implementing a specific 1938 statute under War Department supervision, diff-aware supersession of a named 1935 predecessor withdrawal.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, itemized tract descriptions, diff-aware.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8592—Changing the Name of the Lake Bowdoin Migratory Waterfowl Refuge to Bowdoin National Wildlife Refuge and Adding Certain Lands Thereto

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard wildlife-refuge rename/expansion template (Montana, ~1,398 acres), with a disclosed compatibility proviso preserving continued reclamation and oil/gas-development use pursuant to named statutes.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8593—Prescribing Regulations Governing the Enforcement of the Neutrality of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends an already-existing neutrality-enforcement regulatory regime (EO 8233 of September 5, 1939) to a newly-declared belligerent pair (Italy/Greece) by direct reference, without restating or expanding the underlying regulatory content; implements treaty and law-of-nations obligations following a Presidential neutrality proclamation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear, disclosed legal basis (treaties, law of nations, named proclamation), diff-aware citation to the base order.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Tied to the duration of the named declared war, consistent with the underlying neutrality-enforcement framework.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; extends an established enforcement framework by reference rather than creating new unchecked authority. NOTE: EO 8233 itself is outside this coder's remaining-work scope (part of the ICR validation sample, per the pattern noted for EO 8225/8231), so its own substantive content was not independently re-examined here — flagged for potential future cross-reference if EO 8233 surfaces.

title: Executive Order 8594—Ordering Certain Units and Members of the National Guard of the United States Into the Active Military Service of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Third National Guard federalization order in this stream (matching EO 8530, EO 8551), federalizing a cavalry brigade plus two additional infantry divisions and supporting units on staggered effective dates, pursuant to the same explicit congressional authorization (Public Resolution No. 96) and the 1916 National Defense Act.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, itemized units and members, disclosed staggered effective dates and service terms.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles many named units into one order, but this is a single coherent mobilization action, matching the treatment of EO 8530/EO 8551.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Explicit twelve-consecutive-month term ('unless sooner relieved') — a genuine, disclosed bounding provision, matching EO 8530/EO 8551.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed; implements Congress's own contemporaneous authorization.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Third instance in a now well-established pattern of mass National Guard federalization (EO 8530, EO 8551, and now this order, spanning August-November 1940) — part of the same historically resonant pre-Pearl-Harbor mobilization arc.

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Pure mid-year numeric update to Army enlisted-grade ceiling tables, bounded by named 1940 appropriation acts and expressly including selective-service trainees within the same caps.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8596—Modifying Executive Order No. 3825 of April 14, 1923, and Setting Apart Certain Lands for Aviation Field Purposes

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Narrow, tract-specific modification (Alaska, ~278 acres total) of a standing 1923 townsite reservation to permit Department of Commerce aviation-field use, plus a small adjoining reservation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Clear statutory basis, itemized tract descriptions, diff-aware.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standing reservation 'until revoked by the President or by act of Congress' — matching this stream's convention for ordinary land-reservation orders (not treated as an open-ended discretionary grant given the narrow, single-purpose, tract-specific nature).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No pre-existing oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 8597—Establishing Sitka Naval Airspace Reservation and Kodiak Naval Airspace Reservation

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes exclusive Secretary-of-the-Navy gatekeeping authority over civilian/foreign aircraft entry into two designated Alaska airspace zones, implementing the 1926 Air Commerce Act — a new 'defensive airspace reservation' mechanism paralleling this stream's defensive-sea-area family (EO 8143, EO 8403, EO 8536), restated here as coextensive with two already-existing naval reservations (EO 8216, EO 8278).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Named authority (Secretary of the Navy) and cooperating local law-enforcement officers are disclosed on the order's face.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Enforcement is tied to a specifically-named statute ('penalties prescribed by the Civil Aeronautics Act of 1938'), a more specific citation than the generic 'penalties provided by law' language used in this stream's sea-area-family orders — distinguishing this order and warranting ABSENT rather than PRESENT on this flag.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No expiration or review mechanism — a standing, indefinite-duration exclusion zone, matching the defensive-sea-area family.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sole gatekeeping authority rests with the Secretary of the Navy's own discretion, and the Secretary is further self-authorized to 'prescribe such regulations as may be necessary,' with no other described check.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically resonant national-defense airspace exclusion zone in the pre-Pearl-Harbor Alaska defense buildup, extending existing naval-reservation boundaries (EO 8216, EO 8278) into airspace specifically.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight

title: Executive Order 8612—Amendment of Executive Order No. 8572 of October 21, 1940, Authorizing the Priorities Board and the Administrator of Priorities

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Diff-aware broadening of the Priorities Board/Administrator mechanism first established in EO 8572: expands the priority-requiring authority from deliveries specifically placed under section 2(a) to 'all other naval and Army contracts and orders' generally — a genuine widening of the standing wartime-priorities mechanism over private industry.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The operative 'rules and regulations' continue to require disclosed Presidential approval before taking effect — the same genuine, built-in check carried over from EO 8572.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Acting in the public interest and in the interest of the national defense' continues to supply no defined criteria, now applied to a broadened scope.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No termination date stated; tied to the open-ended national-defense need, matching EO 8572.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Presidential-approval requirement for governing rules and regulations remains a genuine, disclosed check, matching EO 8572.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Continues the direct institutional precursor role to the WWII War Production Board, now with expanded scope covering the entirety of Army/Navy procurement rather than only section 2(a)-specific orders.

EO 8063, EO 8205).

flags.justification: Bypasses competitive examination for these two initial appointments.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Expressly bounded to 'initial appointments' for a newly-created bureau — a genuine, disclosed limiting scope, not an ongoing bypass authority.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
